lebanon.description: Engineer Bassam Bargout passed yesterday, Friday, the anniversary of the Battle of Badr. beatFlagged.headline: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
beatFlagged.description: The BBC sees troops and tanks massing near the border, as speculation mounts that Israel is planning a full-scale ground invasion to fight Hezbollah.

beatFlagged.headline: 'We couldn't sleep because of fear': Residents flee as Israel pounds south Beirut
beatFlagged.description: Communities across huge swathes of Lebanon have been told by Israel to leave their homes due to military action against Hezbollah.

beatFlagged.headline: Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times, satellite image reveals
beatFlagged.description: Iranian authorities say the attack, which took place on Saturday, killed 168 people.

beatFlagged.headline: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
beatFlagged.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

beatFlagged.headline: 'What is the game plan?': The Iran war is unsettling China and its ambitions
'This is war': BBC speaks to Iranians at Iran-Turkey border
beatFlagged.description: BBC correspondent James Waterhouse speaks to people at the border, after US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

beatFlagged.headline: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
beatFlagged.description: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azimi.

beatFlagged.headline: Iran targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq
beatFlagged.description: The Iranian military's strikes come amid speculation that the US wants Iranian Kurdish groups to join its conflict with Iran.

beatFlagged.headline: Israel strikes Beirut after evacuation warning sparks panic
beatFlagged.description: Huge traffic jams form after the unprecedented warning for people to leave the Hezbollah stronghold, as Israel steps up its strikes across Lebanon.

beatFlagged.headline: Checkpoints everywhere and internet blackouts: Life in Iran as US-Israeli strikes intensify
beatFlagged.description: Iranians are coping with daily strikes and security crackdowns - all while trying to stay in touch with loved ones.

beatFlagged.headline: US sub sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean, Hegseth says
beatFlagged.description: More than 100 personnel were thought to be aboard the vessel, which went down to the south of Sri Lanka.

beatFlagged.headline: How depleted weapons stockpiles could affect the Iran conflict
beatFlagged.description: Weapons stocks will not alone decide the outcome of this conflict, but it's certainly a significant factor, writes the BBC's Jonathan Beale.

beatFlagged.headline: US Senate vote fails to rein in Trump war powers on Iran
beatFlagged.description: The measure, passed largely along party lines, would halt US military action until approved by Congress.

beatFlagged.headline: Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as US-Israeli bombardment continues
beatFlagged.description: An official says more preparations are needed for the three-day ceremony in Tehran, where the supreme leader will lie in state.

beatFlagged.headline: Israel tells people in large parts of southern Lebanon to leave ahead of attacks
beatFlagged.description: Civilians are being displaced, taking shelter in tents, parks and cars, as Israel retaliates to Hezbollah rocket attack.
